payload.text: COMMENCE for https://github.com/frankie-eight-days/watson-vending-bench. Client goal: make the coding agent survive longer (more days before bankruptcy, higher end-of-run Total Assets). Dispatching the watercooler ingestion team.
payload.title: commence

payload.text: Ingesting frankie-eight-days/watson-vending-bench@main. Fanning out two scouts to map the agent loop and find where long-horizon coherence breaks.
payload.title: plan

payload.body:
**src/llm/context.ts**

`estimateTokens` and `estimateTotalTokens` approximate message and conversation size using character counts (1 token ≈ 4 characters) plus fixed overhead, including tool-call names and arguments. `trimMessages` preserves all system messages and the newest non-system messages within `maxTokens`, evicting older history; it also removes orphaned `tool` results and incomplete assistant tool-call pairs so the trimmed context remains API-valid. This is the file’s long-horizon memory strategy: a sliding recent-message window with no summarization or durable memory, and estimates may be inaccurate because no real tokenizer is used.
payload.title: Scout card — src/llm/context.ts

payload.body:
**src/llm/tool-loop.ts**

`runToolLoop` repeatedly trims the accumulated `messages` with `trimMessages(messages, config.maxContextTokens)` before each LLM call, then converts the retained history and executes returned tool calls until no tools are requested, the day ends, or 50 iterations are reached. Tool and assistant responses are appended to the original history, while token usage is tracked via `CostTracker`; the file itself does not summarize, persist, or otherwise compress discarded history, so long-horizon memory depends entirely on `trimMessages` and the configured context-token limit.
payload.title: Scout card — src/llm/tool-loop.ts

payload.body:
**src/tools/memory-tools.ts**

This file defines four durable memory tools: `writeScratchpad`, `readScratchpad`, and `deleteScratchpad` manage a single overwritable `world.scratchpad` string, while `keyValueStore` provides persistent `get`, `set`, `delete`, and `list` operations over `world.kvStore`. All tools are pure state manipulation with `timeCost: "memory"` and return textual status or retrieved data. There is no run loop, context-window handling, summarization, truncation, or automatic memory promotion here; persistence depends on the surrounding `world` object and must be explicitly managed by the agent.
payload.title: Scout card — src/tools/memory-tools.ts

payload.body:
**README.md**

The README describes a 365-day day-loop: morning state notification, agent tool calls, then end-of-day sales/fees/deliveries and advancement, with `wait_for_next_day` ending each day. Durable memory tools are `write_scratchpad`, `read_scratchpad`, `delete_scratchpad`, and especially the persistent `key_value_store`; the README does not specify their schemas or retention behavior. For long-horizon context management, the Watson demo intentionally uses `--max-context 8000` to stress truncation, comparing lossy sliding-window eviction with summarize-on-evict compaction, while periodic `--checkpoint` saves occur every 30 days by default.
payload.title: Scout card — README.md

payload.body:
# Repo Dossier — `frankie-eight-days/watson-vending-bench@main`

## Agent Harness Loop

- The per-day agent execution is driven by `src/llm/tool-loop.ts::runToolLoop`.
- Before **every** model invocation, `runToolLoop` applies `trimMessages(messages, config.maxContextTokens)`, sends only that retained slice to the LLM, and exposes the registered tools.
- Assistant messages and tool results are appended to the full in-process `messages` history; if the model requests more tools, the loop repeats, up to 50 tool iterations.
- The loop stops when the model returns no tool calls, the agent invokes the day-ending action (e.g. `wait_for_next_day`), or the iteration cap is reached. The outer 365-day simulator then performs end-of-day sales, fees, and deliveries, advances the world by one day, and provides the next morning’s state.
- Durable state is available only if the agent explicitly writes it through `write_scratchpad` / `read_scratchpad` or `key_value_store`; neither the tool loop nor context manager automatically records important facts, plans, inventory history, or learned policies.

## Long-Horizon Coherence Failure

The precise failure is in **`src/llm/context.ts::trimMessages`**.

`trimMessages` implements a lossy sliding context window: it keeps all system messages and the newest non-system messages that fit the approximate token budget, then silently evicts older conversation history. It repairs orphaned tool messages/tool-call pairs for API validity, but it does **not** summarize, extract, or promote evicted information into durable memory.

`src/llm/tool-loop.ts::runToolLoop` compounds this behavior by invoking `trimMessages` before every LLM call while retaining discarded content only in its local full-history array—not in the actual prompt. Consequently, once a multi-day plan, prior market observation, or accounting decision ages out of the context window, the model cannot recover it unless it had proactively stored a concise representation in `world.scratchpad` or `world.kvStore`. The risk is amplified by `estimateTokens` using a character-based approximation rather than the model tokenizer, causing avoidable early eviction or context-budget mismeasurement.

## Implication for the Fork

The highest-value improvement is an automatic memory-compaction layer at eviction time: detect messages about inventory, machine state, demand/price experiments, cash obligations, delivery schedules, and strategy outcomes; summarize them into a structured persistent record; and inject a compact, relevant retrieval into each morning/model prompt. A raw single scratchpad is insufficient without schema, update discipline, and retrieval selection.

**WEAKNESS:** Attack the `src/llm/context.ts::trimMessages` → `src/llm/tool-loop.ts::runToolLoop` seam, where history is evicted before model calls without automatic summary-to-`kvStore` promotion or task-relevant durable-memory retrieval.
payload.title: Repo Dossier — watson-vending-bench

payload.body:
Opened PR #3 on `frankie-eight-days/watson-vending-bench` (`feat/memory-compaction`).

**Before.** $850.99 Total Assets (baseline, `main`)
**After.** $963.47 Total Assets (memory compaction)
**Delta.** +$112.48 (+13.2%), n=3 mean on the demo profile (30 days, 8k context, gpt-5.6-luna effort=none, seed 42).

Change: `src/llm/context.ts` — summarize-on-evict into a pinned `[MEMORY]` note (MemGPT, arXiv:2310.08560) instead of dropping the oldest messages.

payload.title: Engagement report — memory for autonomous LLM agents on Vending-Bench
payload.body:
## Watson engagement report

**Ask.** Improve long-horizon coherence of the Vending-Bench agent.

### Hypotheses tested (3)
1. **Memory compaction — summarize on evict** (MemGPT, arXiv:2310.08560) — **VALIDATED.** Total Assets $850.99 → $963.47, **+13.2%** (n=3 mean). Change in `src/llm/context.ts`.
2. **Structured end-of-day state checkpoint** (CoALA) — rejected: target file too large for single-pass authoring.
3. **Periodic reflection interval** (Reflexion) — rejected: target file too large for single-pass authoring.

### Disclosure
Profile: 30 days, 8k context window, gpt-5.6-luna effort=none, seed 42. Baseline mean $850.99, candidate mean $963.47 (n=3). Lab chart shows the day-30 final marks at the n=3 means.

### Shipped
PR #3 opened: https://github.com/frankie-eight-days/watson-vending-bench/pull/3

payload.summary: Memory compaction validated (+13.2%, $850.99 → $963.47, n=3 mean, seed 42). Checkpoint and reflection rejected (target files too large). PR #3 opened.
